questions.question: What is an angle?
questions.correct_answer: An angle is formed when two rays originate from a common point called the vertex.
questions.explanation: The rays forming the angle are called arms and the common point is the vertex.

questions.question: What is an acute angle?
questions.correct_answer: An angle whose measure is greater than 0° and less than 90°.
questions.explanation: Acute angles are smaller than a right angle.

questions.question: What is a linear pair of angles?
questions.correct_answer: Two adjacent angles whose non-common arms form a straight line.
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a linear pair is always 180°.

questions.question: What is the sum of angles in a linear pair?
questions.explanation: A ray standing on a line forms two adjacent angles that add up to 180°.

questions.question: What are vertically opposite angles?
questions.correct_answer: Angles opposite each other when two lines intersect.
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are always equal.

questions.question: When are two angles called complementary?
questions.explanation: Complementary angles together form a right angle.

questions.question: When are two angles called supplementary?
questions.explanation: Supplementary angles together form a straight angle.

questions.question: What happens when two lines intersect each other?
questions.correct_answer: They form two pairs of vertically opposite angles.
questions.explanation: Each pair of vertically opposite angles is equal.

questions.question: What is a transversal?
questions.correct_answer: A line that intersects two or more lines at distinct points.
questions.explanation: Transversals form several angle pairs like corresponding and alternate angles.

questions.question: What is the sum of angles around a point?
questions.explanation: All angles around a single point together make a full circle.

notes.title: Types of Angles
notes.content: Angles are classified as acute, right, obtuse, straight and reflex depending on their measure.

notes.title: Linear Pair Axiom
notes.content: If a ray stands on a line, the two adjacent angles formed sum to 180°.

notes.title: Vertically Opposite Angles Theorem
notes.content: When two lines intersect, vertically opposite angles are equal.

notes.title: Parallel Lines
notes.content: Parallel lines never intersect and remain at a constant distance from each other.

notes.title: Angles with Transversal
notes.content: When a transversal cuts parallel lines, corresponding and alternate interior angles are equal.

facts.fact: Angles in a linear pair always add up to 180°.
Vertically opposite angles are always equal.
Angles around a point always sum to 360°.
Lines parallel to the same line are parallel to each other.
